observable_failures: FaceDetector uses static MTCNN parameters with min_face_size=40 pixels and fixed confidence threshold of 0.9
Faces smaller than 40x40 pixels are ignored during detection
Faces detected with confidence score below 0.9 are discarded during post-processing
Low-resolution images result in frequent missed face detections
Small faces occupying a small portion of the frame are overlooked
System does not adapt its sensitivity to input image quality
repair_targets: FaceDetector should dynamically adjust MTCNN parameters (min_face_size, detection thresholds) based on image characteristics
FaceDetector should support intelligent image pre-processing (e.g., super-resolution, adaptive upsampling) before detection
FaceDetector must expose a new public method set_detection_sensitivity(min_face_size_factor, confidence_threshold_offset)
set_detection_sensitivity must update internal detection parameters to reflect desired sensitivity levels
Modifications must not negatively impact performance or accuracy on standard/high-quality images
regression_expectations: Face detection must continue to work correctly on standard or high-quality images with default parameters
Static parameter behavior for non-adaptive use cases must be preserved when no sensitivity adjustment is made

semantic_boundaries: _adjust_detection_parameters(image: PIL.Image.Image) -> (min_face_size: int, confidence_threshold: float) in repo/backend/models/face_detector.py
set_detection_sensitivity(min_face_size_factor: float = 1.0, confidence_threshold_offset: float = 0.0) -> None in repo/backend/models/face_detector.py
behavioral_constraints: TO-BE: add method _adjust_detection_parameters(image, min_face_size_factor, confidence_threshold_offset) -> (min_face_size: int, confidence_threshold: float)
TO-BE: add method set_detection_sensitivity(min_face_size_factor: float = 1.0, confidence_threshold_offset: float = 0.0) -> None
min_face_size_factor must accept values like 0.5 to halve the minimum size (e.g., reducing effective min_face_size from 40 to 20 pixels)
confidence_threshold_offset must accept values like -0.1 to lower the threshold by 0.1 below the base threshold of 0.9
The context manager should maintain consistent suppression behavior when applied to different Qt logger instances — N/A to this issue
Filter pattern matching should properly handle leading whitespace and trailing spaces — N/A to this issue
backward_compatibility: set_detection_sensitivity must use default argument values (min_face_size_factor=1.0, confidence_threshold_offset=0.0) so existing calls without arguments continue to behave identically to the current static-parameter behavior
Existing detection behavior on standard/high-quality images must not be degraded by the addition of adaptive mechanisms
missing_elements_to_implement: Method _adjust_detection_parameters(image: PIL.Image.Image) -> (min_face_size: int, confidence_threshold: float) — declared in new_interfaces.md, does not exist in repo/backend/models/face_detector.py

Method set_detection_sensitivity(min_face_size_factor: float = 1.0, confidence_threshold_offset: float = 0.0) -> None — declared in new_interfaces.md, does not exist in repo/backend/models/face_detector.py
hide_qt_warning must accept multiple loggers (*loggers variadic) — test at line 113 passes 3 args implying this intent
QtWarningFilter.__init__ must accept a logger parameter — tests at lines 98 and 146 pass 2 args

suspect_entities: repo/backend/models/face_detector.py — FaceDetector class (current implementation uses static min_face_size=40 and fixed confidence threshold of 0.9)
MTCNN model initialization in FaceDetector.__init__ or FaceDetector.detect_faces — uses hardcoded parameters
tests/unit/utils/test_qtlog.py — test file with structural corruption and API misuse
qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py — hide_qt_warning and QtWarningFilter have mismatched signatures vs test usage

test_qtlog.py:98 -> qtlog.QtWarningFilter(pattern) — TypeError: 2 args passed, 1 accepted
dataflow_relevant_uses: qtlog.py:232 — assert _args is not None (consumed in qt_message_handler)
qtlog.py:35 — hide_qt_warning accepts (pattern, logger), not 3 args
qtlog.py:46-61 — QtWarningFilter.__init__ accepts only (pattern), not 2 args

must_co_edit_relations: set_detection_sensitivity must be implemented in the same file as FaceDetector class (repo/backend/models/face_detector.py)
_adjust_detection_parameters must be implemented in repo/backend/models/face_detector.py to support set_detection_sensitivity
hide_qt_warning in qtlog.py:35 must be updated to accept *loggers (variadic), and test_qtlog.py:113 updated to match
QtWarningFilter.__init__ in qtlog.py:46 must add logger parameter, and tests at test_qtlog.py:98 and :146 must be updated to match

dependency_propagation: Changes to FaceDetector's internal MTCNN parameter storage (min_face_size, thresholds) affect the actual detection call in detect_faces or __call__
Dynamic parameter adjustment propagates to every face detection invocation using the adapted values
qtlog.hide_qt_warning signature change affects all callers including tests at test_qtlog.py:113
qtlog.QtWarningFilter signature change affects tests at test_qtlog.py:98, :146 and log.py:35-36 re-export
